title: OPSEC Incident Response
when_to_use: Use OPSEC Incident Response to develop professional-grade opsec incident response capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Adversary Infrastructure at Scale pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M07, OM-M13.

steps.title: Detection Mechanisms & Early Warning
steps.instruction: Implement multi-layer detection: automated alerts (certificate reputation warnings, traffic anomalies, team server error rates), human review (log analysis, threat intel cross-referencing), operator reports (suspicious network activity, strange team server behavior). Design early warning system: what signals indicate potential detection?
steps.tip: Early detection buys time. Invest in threat intelligence feeds for malware signatures and C2 domains.

steps.title: Rapid Assessment Protocol
steps.instruction: Upon detection signal, execute rapid assessment: confirm alert legitimacy (avoid false positives), determine scope (single component vs entire infrastructure?), estimate exposure (was operator identified?), timeline (how much was observed?). Assessment must complete in <1 hour. Decision point: is engagement salvageable or total loss?
steps.tip: Assessment answers critical question: do we burn now or investigate further? Wrong choice has high cost.

steps.title: Containment Strategy
steps.instruction: Design containment for various scenarios: team server detected (burn team server + redirect to new), redirector detected (retire + spin up new), C2 malware detected (retire malware variant + recompile), operator identified (safe house relocation + comms change). Containment: rapid, minimal noise, preserves engagement viability where possible.
steps.tip: Containment must be rehearsed. When under pressure, you execute what you practiced.

steps.title: Decision Framework
steps.instruction: Build decision tree: given detection scenario and assessment results, what action: abort engagement, pivot infrastructure, scale down operations, accelerate objectives? Criteria: operator safety, engagement success probability, mission importance, risk tolerance. Document: decision authority, escalation path, communication protocol.
steps.tip: Decision framework should account for incomplete information. Design for worst-case assessment.
steps.expected_output: Decision tree with criteria and authorities

steps.title: Communication & Coordination
steps.instruction: Establish secure communication protocol for incident response: operator notification (encrypted channels), team coordination (command structure), client notification (if appropriate for authorized engagement), evidence preservation (log evidence immediately). Include backup communication channels (air-gapped comms, dead drops if necessary).

steps.title: Post-Incident Review & Improvement
steps.instruction: After any incident (detected or false alarm), conduct thorough review: detection accuracy, assessment quality, containment effectiveness, decision logic, communication clarity. Update threat model, SOPs, training based on lessons. Build continuous improvement loop.
steps.tip: Incidents are expensive; extract maximum learning.
steps.expected_output: Post-incident review template + lessons learned capture
